summary: SQLite store dir created with 0o700 and the .sqlite file (plus WAL/SHM sidecars) chmod'd to 0o600 so local non-owner users cannot dump telemetry, chemistry, equipment topology, command log, or alarm history.
reason: Greptile P1 #3216091791. The SQLite store dir was created with 0o755 and the .sqlite file inherited umask (typically 0o644 on macOS/Linux), letting any local user `sqlite3 store.sqlite` and read the entire history. The cache.go fix from a previous review covered the response cache but missed the store — same sensitivity, same treatment. Fix: MkdirAll mode -> 0o700; after sql.Open succeeds, os.Chmod the SQLite file to 0o600 and conditionally chmod WAL/SHM sidecars (NotExist is non-fatal because sidecars only exist after the first write transaction).
validated_outcome: TestOpenPermissions locks 0o700 dir / 0o600 file using a nested path that exercises the MkdirAll codepath. Verified live: drwx------ store dir, -rw------- store.sqlite after fresh open.
